title: Graduate Business Development Representative (SaaS Sales)
description: We're looking for a curious, ambitious and motivated **Graduate Business Development Representative** to join our Growth team at Alkimii. Looking to kick\-start your career in SaaS sales? We're looking for a curious, ambitious and motivated Graduate Business Development Representative to join our Growth team at Alkimii. You'll learn how modern B2B SaaS sales works from the ground up, working alongside experienced Business Development Executives and Account Executives while building the skills needed to grow into a future commercial leader. **What is Alkimii?** At Alkimii, we're on a mission to transform how hospitality businesses manage their people and property operations. Our platform helps hospitality teams streamline workflows, improve communication and make better operational decisions — all in one place. We're a fast\-growing SaaS company, and our Growth team plays a critical role in how we scale. **The Opportunity** This role is the first step on a structured commercial career path. Over your first year, you'll learn: * Modern SaaS prospecting * Outbound sales * Customer qualification * HubSpot CRM * Hospitality technology * Commercial communication * Pipeline generation You'll work closely with experienced Business Development Executives, Account Executives and Marketing professionals who will help accelerate your development. For high performers, this role provides a clear progression path into a **Business Development Executive (BDE)** position. **What You'll Be Doing** **Prospecting \& Research** • Research hotel groups and hospitality businesses across the UK and Ireland • Identify key decision makers within target accounts • Build prospect lists using LinkedIn, HubSpot and modern prospecting tools • Maintain accurate and structured prospect data **Outreach \& Opportunity Creation** • Use modern sales and AI\-powered prospecting tools to identify opportunities and improve productivity • Engage prospects through phone calls, email and LinkedIn • Introduce Alkimii to potential customers • Generate interest and book qualified meetings • Support outbound campaigns alongside Marketing • Learn and apply best practices in SaaS prospecting **CRM \& Pipeline Management** • Maintain accurate records in HubSpot • Track prospect engagement and activity • Log calls, emails and meetings consistently • Ensure CRM data remains accurate and up to date **Learning \& Development** • Complete Alkimii Academy certification • Shadow Business Development Executives and Account Executives • Participate in call reviews and coaching sessions • Learn objection handling and discovery techniques • Build a strong understanding of our product and industry **What Success Looks Like** **In your first 3 months** • Complete your onboarding and training programme • Develop confidence speaking with prospects • Understand our customers, product and market • Begin generating qualified meetings **In your first 6 months** • Consistently generate qualified meetings • Meet or exceed activity targets • Build strong prospecting habits • Become comfortable managing your own workload **In your first 12 months** • Operate independently • Consistently create pipeline opportunities • Demonstrate strong commercial awareness • Be ready to progress into a Business Development Executive role **We Think You'll Thrive If You:** • Are naturally curious • Enjoy speaking with people • Are resilient and positive • Like working towards goals • Are coachable and eager to learn • Take ownership of your work • Want to build a career in sales, business development or SaaS **Requirements** • Excellent written and verbal communication skills • Strong organisational skills and attention to detail • Comfortable learning new technology and systems • Eligibility to work in Ireland • Fluent English **Nice To Have** • Previous customer\-facing experience • Hospitality experience • Sales, retail or service industry experience • Familiarity with CRM systems • Business, Marketing or related degree **What Can You Expect From Us?** 💰 Competitive salary and commission plan 🎓 Structured training and development programme 🚀 Clear progression path into a Business Development Executive role 🩺 Wellbeing programmes, learning and growth opportunities 🧘 Flexible working week because we take productivity and wellbeing seriously 👩 💻 Hybrid working model 💳 CleverCards employee benefits 🙋 €4,000 Employee Referral Programme 👯 Quarterly team meet\-ups and annual gathering Before You Apply... title: Graduate Environmental Health & Safety (EHS) Advisor
description: **Who We Are** Applied Materials is a global leader in materials engineering solutions used to produce virtually every new chip and advanced display in the world. We design, build and service cutting\-edge equipment that helps our customers manufacture display and semiconductor chips – the brains of devices we use every day. As the foundation of the global electronics industry, Applied enables the exciting technologies that literally connect our world – like AI and IoT. If you want to push the boundaries of materials science and engineering to create next generation technology, join us to deliver material innovation that changes the world. **What We Offer** Salary: €36,000\.00 \- €49,500\.00\&\#xa; Location: Leixlip,IRL You’ll benefit from a supportive work culture that encourages you to learn, develop, and grow your career as you take on challenges and drive innovative solutions for our customers. We empower our team to push the boundaries of what is possible—while learning every day in a supportive leading global company. Visit our Careers website to learn more. At Applied Materials, we care about the health and wellbeing of our employees. We’re committed to providing programs and support that encourage personal and professional growth and care for you at work, at home, or wherever you may go. Learn more about our benefits . We are looking for a Graduate Environmental Health \& Safety Advisor to join our Ireland Field Service Operations team on a **24 month fixed‑term contract** , supporting a major industrial equipment installation and commissioning project in a controlled manufacturing environment *.* **What you will do:** This role is responsible for ensuring that installation and start‑up activities are carried out safely and in full compliance with corporate safety standards, regulatory requirements, and project‑specific controls. The Graduate Environmental Health \& Safety Advisor will work closely with project teams, contractors, and Environmental, Health \& Safety (EHS) partners to promote a strong safety culture and reduce risk throughout the project lifecycle. This position requires a visible, hands‑on safety presence at our customer site in **Leixlip, Co. Kildare,** and the ability to operate effectively in a fast‑paced, high‑risk project environment. **Role Responsibilities** * Carry out daily site safety walks and follow up on observations and corrective actions * Support site safety inductions and onboarding for new starters and contractors * Assist with pre‑task planning, job hazard analysis, and dynamic risk assessments * Support permit‑to‑work activities (e.g. LOTO, confined spaces, working at heights) * Monitor compliance with safety procedures, PPE requirements, and site rules * Support incident and near‑miss investigations and corrective actions * Deliver toolbox talks and safety briefings as required * Prepare weekly and monthly safety reports and track safety KPIs * Support project readiness reviews, key installation milestones, and start‑up activities * Act as a link between project teams, EHS, and on‑site service partners **Minimum Qualifications** * Recognised Health \& Safety qualification at NFQ Level 7 or equivalent ((e.g., NEBOSH, IOSH, or equivalent) * Strong communication and safety documentation skills * Ability to work effectively in a fast‑paced, high‑risk project environment * Hands‑on, proactive approach with a visible on‑site presence **Why Join This Project?** * Be part of a large‑scale, high‑visibility industrial project with strong safety leadership and clear standards * Gain hands‑on experience supporting complex equipment installation and commissioning activities * Work in a collaborative, on‑site environment alongside experienced project, engineering, and EHS professionals * Opportunity to make a real impact on safety performance and culture **Additional Information** **Time Type:** Full time **Employee Type:** Fixed Term (Fixed Term) **Travel:** No **Relocation Eligible:** No The salary offered to a selected candidate will be based on multiple factors including location, hire grade, job\-related knowledge, skills, experience, and with consideration of internal equity of our current team members. In addition to a comprehensive benefits package, candidates may be eligible for other forms of compensation such as participation in a bonus and a stock award program, as applicable. For all sales roles, the posted salary range is the Target Total Cash (TTC) range for the role, which is the sum of base salary and target bonus amount at 100% goal achievement. Applied Materials is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, national origin, citizenship, ancestry, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, veteran or military status, or any other basis prohibited by law.

title: Group Financial Accountant
description: **Junior Group Financial Accountant \- 12 month FTC** **Location:** Dublin or Carrick\-on\-Shannon, Co. Leitrim (hybrid – minimum 3 days per week in the office) **WHO WE ARE** Cora Systems is one of the fastest\-growing B2B SaaS businesses in Europe and the US, with offices in Ireland \& US. Cora, a Gartner Magic Quadrant supplier, enables companies to manage projects across complex value chains profitably and counts a number of Fortune 100 companies as its customers. We have a strong company culture of openness and transparency. We aim for a fun working environment where our people enjoy their work and strive to do their very best for our clients and the business. **ROLE PURPOSE / OVERVIEW** The Junior Group Financial Accountant sits at the heart of our finance function, owning the core operational finance processes that keep a fast\-growing international SaaS Group running smoothly. This is a broad, hands\-on role spanning the month\-end close and management accounts, order\-to\-cash and billing, cash flow forecasting, payroll coordination, and statutory and revenue reporting across our Irish \& US entities.You will work closely with the Financial Controller, the Head of FP\&A and the wider finance team, as well as with Customer Success, Sales, Professional Services and HR, ensuring our financial data is accurate, timely and insightful. The role offers significant ownership and visibility, with genuine opportunity to improve and digitalise processes, including through the application of AI tools, as the business scales. **KEY RESPONSIBILITIES** **Management Accounts \& Month\-End Close** * Own key elements of the month\-end close across our Irish and US entities * Prepare deferred schedules, fully reconciled to NetSuite at entity and consolidated level * Complete balance sheet reconciliations and aged debtor/creditor reporting **Order\-to\-Cash \& Billing** * Manage customer invoicing in NetSuite, ensuring invoices and POs are raised accurately and on time * Maintain the ARR register and partner with Sales and Customer Success to keep customer data clean **Cash Flow Forecasting** * Maintain core inputs to the Group cash flow forecast, including ARR, services, staff costs, vendors and FX * Reconcile forecast to actuals monthly, monitor liquidity, and challenge vendor costs and payment terms **ARR \& Revenue Reporting** * Maintain the recurring revenue register and reporting of ARR movements, renewal and metrics **Payroll \& Pensions** * Coordinate Global payroll, including reviews, journals and pension submissions **Statutory, Tax \& Other Reporting** * Prepare Irish VAT returns, Revenue (ROS) submissions and statutory survey returns **Travel \& Expenses (“T\&E")** * Consolidate and report monthly T\&E to the Senior Management Team **Process Improvement \& AI** * Identify opportunities to automate and digitalise finance processes, leveraging AI tools and NetSuite functionality **REQUIRED EXPERIENCE** * Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or a related field; MBA or relevant advanced degree preferred * 3\+ years of progressive experience in a Financial Accounting role, ideally in a fast passed SaaS or technology environment * Part\-qualified or Qualified accountant (ACA, ACCA, CIMA, CPA or equivalent) * Demonstrated experience in a similar industry finance role covering month\-end close, reconciliations and billing/order\-to\-cash processes * Demonstrated experience leveraging AI tools to improve efficiency, reporting, analysis or business processes * Experience using ERP systems (NetSuite preferred) **SKILLS \& COMPETENCIES** * Strong Excel skills, including working confidently with formula\-driven, multi\-tab financial models * Understanding of AI tools and their practical application within finance and business processes * Strong financial and business acumen, with the ability to see the commercial story behind the numbers * Solid technical accounting knowledge, particularly around revenue recognition, accruals, prepayments and balance sheet reconciliation * Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple recurring deadlines (month\-end, payroll, VAT, surveys) in parallel * Clear and confident written and verbal communication skills, comfortable engaging with non\-finance stakeholders **PERSONAL ATTRIBUTES** * Self\-starter with strong initiative * Confident communicator * Strong attention to detail * Commercially minded * Ability to work independently and take ownweship * Strong problem\-solving skills * Adaptable and comfortable in a fast\-paced environment

Dalata successfully operate Ireland’s two largest hotel brands, the Clayton and the Maldron Hotels across Ireland, the UK and mainland Europe, as well as managing a small portfolio of partner hotels. You must be a detailed\-oriented, multi\-tasker with a burning desire to contribute. **Benefits** * Free employee meals on duty * 50% Off FB in all properties * €/£60 employee rate on rooms for all Dalata\-owned hotels * 30% off Friends Family rate * Extra AL day for every year worked (5 days to a maximum of 5 days) * Employee Assistance Programme. * Cycle to Work Scheme/Transport Schemes * SAYE (Save As You Earn Scheme) * Pension access (Ask your HR Manager for local pension information) * Development Opportunities through our Dalata Academy\- support your career journey * Staff Appreciation Initiatives, fun team\-building activities and regular charity events **Your Job:** * Responsible for compiling and publishing weekly internal vacancies for the group consisting of over 56 hotels. * Support the Group Talent Acquisition Manager with virtual careers fairs, open days, day\-to\-day recruitment support to the hotels and any daily recruitment queries in a timely manner. * Maintain the Applicant Tracking System ensuring all roles are being posted correctly, onto the right platforms and enhancement of the candidate experience and employer brand by ensuring all candidates are being progressed through the system to ensure accurate reporting. * Manage support the Graduate recruitment process and collaborate with Dalata Academy team * Assist the Group Talent Acquisition Manager with New Build and Acquisitions sourcing and recruitment plans. * Support hotels in sourcing candidates for any management or specialists gaps. * Prepare stats, reports and metrics to assist the Group Talent Acquisitions Manager. * Assist with projects as required **What You’ll Need:** * 2 year's\+ experience in HR/Recruitment in a fast\-paced environment experience would be an advantage * Excellent Knowledge of MS Office (Outlook, Word, PowerPoint and Excel) * Excellent communication, interpersonal and time management skills * Adaptable and flexible with a positive can\-do attitude * Ability to manage high workloads and conflicting priorities while maintaining confidential information * Experience using and managing a ATS or recruitment tools would be an advantage * Good collaborator and able to demonstrate how they have doe this across multi site **About Us** Dalata Hotel Group – We bring *the heart of hospitality* to every hotel we own and operate. We see our growth and expansion plans as a way of spreading our unique way of caring for our guests further around the world, bringing better experiences to more people. Currently, the Dalata difference can be felt at each of our hotels in Ireland, the UK, Germany the Netherlands, and we’ll keep expanding this further, focusing on key UK cities, Dublin and Continental Europe. Helping our people to grow and develop their careers is vitally important to us and is one of our highest priorities. We have many exciting and unique internal development programmes, each crafted to make sure our employees get the learning they need to be able to take the next step in their careers. Dalata Hotel Group Plc is an Inclusive Employer. We actively encourage people from all backgrounds and abilities to apply to be part of our team. However, we understand everyone's needs are different; therefore, if you require any accommodations with this application process, please highlight your needs when you are completing your application process. Dalata Hotel Group is Ireland’s largest hotel group. We’re excited to be rapidly growing in the UK and have recently expanded into Continental Europe. We have a large portfolio of wonderful hotels in over 17 cities, making Dalata Hotel Group your ideal choice for your next role in hospitality. At Dalata, our ambition is to be as welcoming and rewarding to our staff as we are to our guests.

The ideal candidate will have a strong work ethic, good route knowledge, and a commitment to high standards of safety and customer service. **Key Responsibilities:** * Carry out scheduled deliveries across Ireland during night hours. * Ensure timely and accurate delivery of goods to customer locations. * Complete all necessary paperwork, including delivery dockets and timesheets. * Carry out pre\- and post\-trip vehicle checks and report any issues. * Maintain a clean and professional appearance of both yourself and your vehicle. * Adhere to all driving regulations, company procedures, and road safety laws. * Communicate effectively with the Transport Office regarding delays, route issues, or delivery updates. * Ensure secure loading and unloading of goods, maintaining load safety throughout the journey. * Represent the company in a professional and courteous manner at all times. **Requirements:** * Full clean **Category C\+E (Artic) Licence**. * Valid **CPC card** and **Digital Tachograph card**. * Strong knowledge of Irish roads and delivery routes. * Good communication and time management skills. * Ability to work night shifts and flexible hours when required. * Reliability and a strong safety\-first mindset. **Desirable:** * Experience with refrigerated or multi\-drop deliveries. * Previous experience delivering to RDCs or retail depots. title: HR Administrator
description: Alltec Fibre Ltd is a leading specialist in fibre optic network design, installation, and maintenance. Established in 1998 and headquartered in Dublin, we deliver high\-quality services across telecoms, utilities, transportation, data centres, and government sectors throughout Europe and the Middle East. ***Job Overview*** We are seeking an ambitious and motivated Human Resources Administrator to join our team in Dublin, Ireland. title: HR Graduate Aministrator Employee Benefits - German speaker
description: At MiniMed, you can begin a lifelong career of exploration and innovation, while helping make a difference in the lives of people living with diabetes around the globe. You'll lead with purpose, breaking down barriers to innovation for a more connected, compassionate world. **About The Role** This is an exciting time to join MiniMed as our teams continue to expand and we experience growth. Our remote team in the UK have an exciting opportunity for an HR graduate to join us in Ireland, as the team continue to provide a world class service in our different regions. If you are a dedicated, individual who thrives on building relationships and providing excellent customer service and working at pace with our employees, this is an opportunity worth exploring. **Responsibilities may include the following and other duties may be assigned:** * Serve as the primary point of contact for employees, managers, and HR partners regarding local benefits, leave eligibility, processes, and timelines. * Respond in a timely manner to compensation and benefits related queries through our HRIS system, Workday. * Administer and manage all local benefit and employee leave programs, including but not limited to medical, retirement, family care leave, medical leave, parental leave, and disability\-related absences. * Coordinate with third\-party leave administrators and insurance carriers to ensure accurate case management, documentation, and benefit continuation. Ensure all employees are enrolled in relevant employee benefits, help provide data for audits. * Proactively support absence management (long term illness, maternity, paternity) with HR partners to ensure return to work processes are followed and HRIS system is updated. * Have an awareness of local regulations and defined company policies. * Ensure accurate continuation, billing, and reconciliation and payment of benefits and payment to benefits providers. * Closely partner with payroll to ensure correct pay, deductions, and adjustments for benefits and leave. * Support defining system requirements that facilitate efficiency, automation, and self\-service for various HR processes. **Required Knowledge And Experience** * Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources or Business. * Experience in HR, compensation, or benefits administration is preferred * Experience working with HRIS (Workday) is advantageous * Biligual \- fluent German preferred **Skills And Competencies** * Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple cases simultaneously * Strong interpersonal and communication skills with a customer\-focused mindset. * Ability to handle sensitive information with discretion and confidentiality. * Analytical, agile and adaptable with good problem\-solving skills. * Ability to interpret policies and regulations and apply them consistently. * Comfortable working independently while collaborating with cross\-functional teams. \#BetterDaysStartNow **Physical Job Requirements** The above statements are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by employees assigned to this position, but they are not an exhaustive list of all the required responsibilities and skills of this position. **Benefits \& Compensation** **MiniMed offers a competitive salary and flexible benefits package** At MiniMed, we put people first. A commitment to our employees lives at the core of our values: We recognize their contributions. They share in the success they help create. We offer a wide range of benefits, resources, and competitive compensation plans designed to support you at every stage of your career and life. **About MiniMed** We want to make every day a better day for people living with diabetes. Our team of creative innovators around the globe share a passion for finding the simplest solutions to the problems that people with diabetes face on a daily basis. For more than 40 years, we've been redefining what's possible, from intelligent dosing systems designed for real life to predictive insights that stay a step ahead, and we're dedicated to continuing to support our customers through every step of their journey — meeting them where and how they need it.

title: HR Officer
description: **Description** Ryanair are currently recruiting for a **HR Officer** to join Europe’s Largest Airline Group. This is an excellent opportunity to join as we look to grow to over 800 aircraft and 300m guests within the next 10 years. The life of a HR Officer at Ryanair can vary quite a bit! They are the face of our HR Team and will be on hand to support our 27,000\+ aviation professionals across our growing European network. The HR Officer deals with a broad range HR issues and administration, from legal cases to drafting contracts and all the stuff in between, while travelling frequently to your assigned bases to meet with employees and answer an queries they may have. At Ryanair, you will gain vast experience very early in your career as you are entrusted and expected to act like a HR Manager for the 1000\+ employees under your remit to make daily decisions, offer advice and resolve all employee relations matters that arise. This is a perfect opportunity for a recent graduate seeking a challenge and to join a fast paced and dynamic working environment. **Duties Include** * Managing the entire HR function for up to 1000\+ employees in your assigned bases/ areas. * Monitoring employee absence and turnover to identify trends and deal with same. * Liaising, supporting and advising management at all levels across the business on all HR related matters, ranging from general queries, grievances to disciplinary investigations and legal case preparation. * Maintain the HR databases to ensure all files and the HR system is up to date and accurate. * Weekly reporting to the HR Manager on all HR issues in your area * Contract, payroll \& reference etc. administration. * Good knowledge of business and HR model to answer daily employee queries and make informed decisions. * Excellent writing skills to be able to compose important disciplinary etc. letters. * Working on ad hoc projects as and when they arise. * Legal case preparation for local litigation across Europe and for WRC \& LC. * Travelling across Europe to meet with employees on general employment matters, attend interviews and employee negotiations. **Requirements** * Relevant HR or business degree is essential * Ability to work in a pressurised and fast paced environment * Strong organisational skills * Strong interpersonal skills * The ability to work on own initiative * Excellent verbal and written communication skills * The ability to work under tight deadlines effectively and efficiently * PC Skills (Word, Excel, Power point etc) **Benefits** * Competitive salary * Discounted and unlimited travel to over 250 destinations * Defined Contribution Pension Scheme * Death in Service Benefit – Up to 2 times of annual basic salary * 20 Days Annual Leave – Increasing to 22 days after 12 months and 25 days after 3 years of continuous service. * Option for up to 5 additional unpaid leave days per year * Cycle 2 Work Scheme * Unrivalled career progression Competencies

title: HV Transmission Engineer
description: At Mace, our purpose is to redefine the boundaries of ambition. We believe in creating places that are responsible, bringing transformative impact to our people, communities, and societies across the globe. To learn more about our purpose, culture, and priorities, visit our strategy site. Within our consult business, we harness our unique combination of leading\-edge practical expertise and project delivery consultancy to unlock the potential in every project. The project: The role is within the technical design authority for the transmission system. Engineering and asset management is the area that facilitates that role through the development of technical standards and functional specifications for all plants to be connected to the transmission system and the development of new and innovative technologies and methodologies. In addition, the area is responsible for ensuring that requirements are incorporated in project design and construction whether the works are carried out by ESB networks or by third parties with medium voltage and high voltage projects. Our values shape the way we consult, and define the people we want to join us on our journey:* Safety first + Going home safe and well * Client focus \- Deliver on our promise * Integrity \- Always do the right thing * Create opportunity + for our people to excel You will be responsible for: * Ensuring that technical queries raised during the project are dealt with in accordance with TSO standards and policies and are closed out efficiently. * Ensuring that projects are carried out in a manner that meets requirements. * Reviewing and comparing submitted detailed designs against functional requirements and outline designs. * Monitoring of construction progress, and onsite inspection to ensure compliance with standards, functional requirements, and outline designs. * Ensuring developments are commissioned to comply with the requirements by producing and issuing commissioning instructions * Verifying transmission system requirements are met to allow commissioning tests to proceed. * Assisting the lead engineers with the engagement with customer and stakeholder technical consultants as and when required in order to resolve issues arising. * Coordinating with peers to meet business priorities, manage issues as they arise, provide technical guidance, and act as an escalation point to EAM manager. * Managing interactions on own projects with client engineering vendors, customers, and internal stakeholders. * Providing technical direction and assistance across the organisation on own projects and to team members and junior staff, and act as a team representative when required. * Ensuring the completion of stage gate reviews on all customer projects to ensure that the requirements of functional specifications are met. * Providing completion and dissemination of lessons learned on each project post project energisation. * Ensuring appropriate engagement with our partners in ESB networks including meetings, reporting, relationship management, and general communication as applicable to the client engineering role. * Facilitating and inputting project reviews, assessment, and auditing by internal and external parties as required. You’ll need to have: * You have an electrical engineering degree or equivalent with suitable experience within MV/HV projects. * You have transmission substation design expertise with experience assessing substation designs for conformity to standards at 110/220/400 kV (MV, HV). * You are digitally savvy, can build lasting client relationships, and lead strong, motivated teams. * You will have a construction or project management background and be hungry to grow your career and enjoy the opportunities Mace has to offer. You will have experience completing onsite inspections and monitoring construction progress for compliance to ensure transmission assets can be commissioned in accordance with standards. * Experience preparing and issuing transmission design drawings, such as single line diagrams, substation layout drawings, protection schematics and interlocking drawings. You’ll also have: * Membership of RICS, CIOB, APM, ICE or equivalent demonstrable professional or personal development. * Substantial experience in the successful delivery phase of projects and programmes in the energy sector. * Property or infrastructure project management expertise. * Commercial and financial acumen. * Experience of leading parts of projects within large, diverse teams. * Experience of developing and forming relationships with senior stakeholders.Knowledge of how carbon and sustainability considerations will shape the outlook of the projects you are working on. Mace is an inclusive employer and welcomes interest from a diverse range of candidates. Even if you feel you do not fulfil all of the criteria, please apply as you may still be the best candidate for this role or another role within our organization. \#LI\-Hybrid

title: Hadoop Software Engineer
description: At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts. Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet. Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all. We are part of the Analytics Data Platform Team within eBay, working with a mission towards improving availability, security, performance, tools and other features within Analytics Platform, specifically on Hadoop. We are looking for hardworking engineers who have a real passion for building and shipping great software especially with open source products and leading and contributing to transformational projects around Hadoop and related products. You will be responsible for developing features and frameworks with the focus on scenarios over Hadoop, Spark, and Databases. eBay has one of the largest Hadoop Deployments in the world with over 18500\+ nodes and 500\+ Petabytes of configured storage. Desirable backgrounds would include strong Java development with technical experience on Big Data stack, with passion for innovating in and around Big Data ecosystems. **The Cool Part** * Be part of the mission to contribute into and pave the path for the next generation Hadoop Platform at eBay. * Work in small groups of extremely hardworking and dedicated engineers in a fast\-paced agile environment. * Design and implement differentiated production\-grade features related to security, high availability, and multi\-tenancy. * Contribute to open source projects while delivering value to eBay **Responsibilities** * Lead and participate in the design and implementation of our Analytics infrastructure to align with the vision of eBay Platform Infrastructure. * Focus on innovative ideas to drive the future of open source technology for patent filing and open source community contribution. * Design and implement technical solutions to support the ecosystem of Analytics Infrastructure. * Work with a team of engineering expertise to design and develop innovative technical solutions. **Job Requirements** * Proven successful track record of 3\+ years of software engineering design and development experience with a strong background working with open source technology. * Strong development skills in Java with commensurate abilities in designing performance, scalability, concurrency, and extensibility. * Have passion and strong engineering background in dealing with big data in the e\-Commerce industry. * Excellent background in computer science fundamentals, data structures, and algorithms. * Excellent problem solving and analytical skill on both defect and performance issue resolution. * Proven result oriented individual with the delivery focus in a high velocity, high\-quality environment. * Experience in Hadoop ecosystems such as Hadoop, Spark, HBase, Hive, etc. **Basic Qualifications** * Master Degree in Computer Science or a bachelor’s degree with significant proven experience is required * 3\+ years related work experience * Strong Java development experience * Good Linux and networking knowledge * Disciplined work style requiring minimum supervision * Strong technical and large systems design skills * Strong troubleshooting and problem\-solving skills * Strong verbal and written interpersonal skills **Benefits** Benefits are an essential part of your total compensation for the work you do every day. Whether you’re single, in a growing family, or nearing retirement, eBay offers a variety of comprehensive and competitive benefit programs to meet your needs. Including maternal \& paternal leave, paid sabbatical, and plans to help ensure your financial security today and in the years ahead because we know feeling financially secure during your working years and through retirement is important. Here at eBay, we love creating opportunities for others by connecting people from widely diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and geographies. So, being diverse and inclusive isn’t just something we strive for, it is who we are, and part of what we do each and every single day. We want to ensure that as an employee, you feel eBay is a place where, no matter who you are, you feel safe, included, and that you have the opportunity to bring your unique self to work. To learn about eBay’s Diversity \& Inclusion click here: https://www.ebayinc.com/company/diversity\-inclusion/ **Additional Details** eBay is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, and disability, or other legally protected status. If you have a need that requires accommodation, please contact us at talent@ebay.com. We will make every effort to respond to your request for accommodation as soon as possible. View our accessibility statement to learn more about eBay's commitment to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities. We use cookies to enhance your experience and may use AI tools for administrative tasks in the hiring process. To learn how we handle your personal data and use AI responsibly, please visit our Talent Privacy Notice, Privacy Center, and AI Hiring Guidelines.

title: Housekeeper
description: Ensures the overall cleanliness and proper appearance of the community, including external litter, all internal communal spaces, reception, designated resident rooms, office areas, and other amenities to enhance and maintain the community appeal and provide a positive resident experience. ***Key Role Responsibilities*** * Work as part of a friendly and supportive team playing a vital role within the Community team to ensure all our residents enjoy a high level of cleanliness throughout their community. * Responds positively to customer queries, resolving them in a timely manner or signposting residents to the most appropriate person to assist them. * Cleans the reception, offices, common spaces and restrooms. * Checks on a daily basis all amenity areas and cleans where required. * Carries out a full litter pick daily to all external areas in the community. * Ensures all empty rooms and apartment and targeted show flats are freshened throughout the day to maintain market\-ready appeal. * Conducts weekly flush downs in all empty rooms and apartments in line with health and safety policies. * Assists in the make\-ready process by performing additional duties requested by Community Manager and/or supervisor. * Informs Community Manager of any observed hazard or potentially dangerous situation to residents, team members, guests, and others. * Logs maintenance issues with the Customer Service team where identified as part of daily tasks and routine. * Ad hoc requests to address Resident concerns or queries Maintains an appropriate inventory of cleaning supplies and informs the appropriate supervisor when a re\-order is necessary. * Makes best use of materials and equipment provided championing cost efficiencies whenever possible without compromising on quality. * Maintaining agreed cleaning standards within designated resident rooms, including but not limited to, kitchens and bathrooms, vacuuming carpets, sweeping and mopping floors, dusting and polishing furniture, and emptying trash containers. You may also be asked to change bed linen, make beds, replace used towels, and restock bathroom supplies like shampoo and soap, if required. * Maintain and manage agreed stock levels, organised appropriately to avoid any damaged linen. ***About You*** You should Demonstrated the ability to solve problems in various practical situations while adhering to standardized procedures showcases strong attention to detail and flexibility in a changing environment. Proven capacity to work autonomously, using initiative as needed, and effectively interact with residents, clients, and team members highlights proficiency in customer service and interpersonal communication. Upholding integrity by adhering to the highest ethical standards and showing honesty and humility, while maintaining accountability for actions and results, reflects a commitment to professionalism. Presenting a positive, dignified image and working collaboratively with others to achieve goals emphasizes teamwork. Prioritizing service by dedicating time, knowledge, and experience to meet the needs of customers, community, and team members underscores a strong service orientation. ***What We Offer*** *We include a range of benefits for our corporate team members, including competitive pension, healthcare, and holiday allowance, amongst others as standard. Please reach out if you are keen to know more.* ***About Greystar*** *Greystar is a leading, fully integrated real estate company offering expertise in investment management, development, and management of rental housing properties globally. The company's business model is unique in its ability to own, operate, and develop multifamily, student, and senior housing across the globe. Building a global platform with a local presence, Greystar continues to expand its geographic reach around the world, bringing a globally interconnected professional rental housing platform and industry funded with institutional capital to countries where the sector does not yet exist. Greystar’s pan – European platform was established in 2013 and has scaled rapidly, now with investment, operational and development presence in the UK, Ireland, France, the Netherlands, Germany, Austria, and Spain.* *One of Greystar’s core values is Equality, and as such, we continue to pride ourselves on being an equal opportunities employer, accounting for all protected and identifiable characteristics. We truly value diversity of thought, background and of experience. Please reach out to the Talent Acquisition team to explore opportunities at Greystar in more detail or visit our careers site at https://jobs.greystar.com/ .*

title: Information Technology System Administrator
description: **Systems Administrator (Azure)** **Dublin South West** **Onsite** **€60K plus DOE** MPG Recruitment are thrilled to be partnering with a well\-established and growing Irish company who are expanding their IT team and looking to hire a talented Systems Administrator with solid Azure experience. **The role** This is a hands\-on, primarily on\-site position based in Dublin South West. Reporting to the Cloud Manager, you will be responsible for maintaining and supporting both on\-premises and Azure cloud environments, providing technical support to internal staff and external customers, and ensuring infrastructure availability, performance, and security. On\-call is one week in three, with an appropriate allowance and compensation provided. **What you'll need** * 3\+ years of experience in a Systems Administration or infrastructure role * Strong Windows Server administration skills (Active Directory, DNS, DHCP, Group Policy) * Solid Azure experience — VMs, VNETs, Azure AD, Storage, Backup, RBAC * PowerShell or Bash scripting — essential * Networking fundamentals — firewalls, VPNs, routing, protocols * Microsoft 365 administration including Exchange Online * Strong troubleshooting and communication skills **Desirable but not essential** * IT degree or relevant qualification * Azure certification (any level considered a benefit) * Some Linux experience (CentOS or Debian) * Familiarity with security frameworks (ISO 27001, NIST) * Experience with monitoring tools (Nagios, PRTG or similar) If this sounds like the right move for you, we'd love to hear from you. Email Margare **t at margaret@mpgrecruitment.ie**

title: Inventory Control Associate
description: Here at Americold Dublin we are now recruiting for a Stock Office Administrator. We are seeking a detail\-oriented and organized individual to join our team as an Stock office Administrator. In this role, you will oversee stock movements, maintain accurate inventory records, and ensure seamless communication with customers and internal teams. This position involves managing and controlling stock movements and balances for customers under your supervision, ensuring accuracy in inventory levels and transactions for effective stock management. You will accurately and promptly record stock movements, changes in product status, and other inventory matters in the Warehouse Management System (WMS). You will coordinate and document daily stock\-related transactions to ensure smooth operations. Collaborating with Stores personnel is crucial to planning, receiving, and delivering customer inventory. Additionally, you will provide customers with regular reports and updates as agreed. In this role, you will handle customer telephone inquiries professionally and resolve any stock\-related concerns. Another key responsibility is daily updates to customer stock systems to ensure data accuracy. Attention to detail and strong organizational skills are essential for this role. Proficiency in Warehouse Management Systems or similar inventory software is necessary. Effective communication skills for liaising with customers and internal teams and the ability to manage multiple tasks and prioritize efficiently are also required. This role is vital for maintaining customer satisfaction and operational efficiency through precise stock management and proactive communication. If you are organized and customer\-focused, we encourage you to apply. **Working Hours** Monday to Thursday. 12pm \- 9pm **Key requirements** * Previous experience with warehouse inventory * Excellent working knowledge of a Warehouse Management System (WMS) * A self\-starter \- this role has a high\-volume turnaround * Good communication skills (both verbal and written) * Good problem\-solving skills * Flexible during times of change and display a willingness to take on additional duties as required within the team * Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment

title: Investment Analyst
description: **We are partnering with an international investment platform to appoint an Analyst within its Real Estate Credit team, based in Dublin.** **This is a high\-profile opportunity to join a growing platform with a strong track record, offering exposure to complex lending transactions across Europe.** **Key Responsibilities** * Support the underwriting and financial modelling of commercial real estate debt transactions * Assist in the evaluation of new investment opportunities across EMEA * Coordinate due diligence processes, working with internal stakeholders and external advisors * Contribute to investment memoranda and committee materials * Participate in deal structuring, negotiation and execution **Candidate Profile** * 1–3 years’ experience within real estate lending, structured finance, or a related field * Strong financial modelling and analytical capability * Solid understanding of commercial real estate and credit fundamentals * Clear, concise communicator with strong written and presentation skills * Highly motivated, detail\-oriented and capable of operating in a fast\-paced environment **To discuss in confidence, contact Diarmuid Clancy via dclancy@elevatepartners.ie or apply directly via the link provided.**

title: Investment Analyst Venture Capital
description: An established Irish Venture Capital firm with a strong track record across equity and lending investments is seeking to hire an **Investment Executive** to join its growing team. This is an exciting opportunity for a commercially minded individual to join the investment team at a key growth stage. The role offers broad exposure across transactions, portfolio management, fundraising and investor engagement, and is well suited to someone looking to build long\-term experience across multiple asset types. **Role Breakdown:** * Full investment lifecycle (origination to completion) * Deal sourcing and screening * Due diligence and investment analysis * Investment committee preparation * Transaction execution and completion * Portfolio monitoring and value\-add support * Investor reporting and communications * Fundraising and business development activity **Ideal candidate:** * Venture Capital Exposure, Corporate Finance, Deals, Transaction Services, Investment Banking, Private Equity. * Contribute meaningfully to investment evaluation and execution * Build trusted relationships with portfolio companies and ecosystem partners * Deliver high\-quality analysis, memos and reporting consistently * Demonstrate sound judgement, curiosity and an investor mindset If you are interested in discussing this role, please reach out to Dylan Philpott at d.philpott@masonalexander.ie

title: Investment Associate
description: **A growing renewable energy investment platform is hiring an Investment Associate to support acquisitions and development\-stage investments across European infrastructure technologies.** The role offers exposure across the full investment lifecycle — origination, financial modelling, due diligence, transaction execution and project finance — working closely with senior investment teams. **Responsibilities** * Evaluate and originate renewable energy investment opportunities * Build and review acquisition and valuation models * Support commercial, technical and legal due diligence * Assist with transaction execution (acquisitions, JVs, financings) * Prepare investment materials for internal committees **Requirements** * 3\+ years’ experience in IB, Big 4 (Deals / Valuations), or renewables development * Strong financial modelling and valuation skills * Transaction exposure in infrastructure or energy * Degree\-qualified (finance, engineering or similar) **To discuss this opportunity, contact Diarmuid Clancy via dclancy@elevatepartners.ie or apply directly via the link provided.**

title: Investor Relations Associate  (Alternative Fund Services - TA)
description: **Job Description** Job Summary: As an Investor Relations Associate in Alternative Fund Services Investor Relations department, you will lead the delivery of investor relations services to clients across Alternative Investment Services. You will collaborate closely with senior team members to ensure client service excellence, lead in day\-to\-day operations, and contribute to process improvements. This role is ideal for candidates with knowledge and seek to grow their career in alternative investment service, with a passion on growth, leadership, teamwork, and operational efficiency. The role entails from capturing, reviewing subscriptions, redemptions and capital calls, cash management, investor/client query management, and support internal departments to service client base and implement change to deliver the best results for our business. As part of this talented team, you will continue to develop your career, learn about the Investor Relations and always be “Audit Ready”. **Job Responsibilities** * Work on a team of highly dedicated individuals to ensure accurate and timely processing of all Fund and Investor level activity (Dealing, Cash, Settlement, Reporting, Query Management,) * Serve as the main contact for a portfolio of clients; answer all phone calls and emails pertaining to those relationships and demonstrate ability to resolve inquiries quickly and accurately. * Perform completeness reviews in a quality assurance capacity as it relates to investor or fund activity/changes; ensure all work is ‘Always Audit Ready' * Oversee and review client and investor level reporting, such as monthly/quarterly statements, contract notes, order acknowledgements, and regulatory reports * Manage the final signoff process for investor transactions and resolve any outstanding documentation * Follow JPMorgan's established processes and procedures and modify/iterate processes and procedures to continually improve accuracy, reduce redundancy, and mitigate risk **Required Qualifications, Capabilities, And Skills** * Minimum 3\+ years' experience in the Financial Services industry, preferably experience with a Fund Administrator, Hedge Fund, or Private Equity Fund * Bachelor's degree in finance, economics, accounting or any relevant field. * Dedicated to superior client service and able to remain poised under pressure * Posses strong communication skills both oral and written * Excellent interpersonal skills * Ability to work with a high level of precision in a fast\-paced, deadline\-driven environment * Ability to collaborate and solve problems that involve multiple stakeholders * Ability to work independently with strong follow\-through **Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities, And Skills** * Master's degree in finance, economics, accounting or any relevant field. * Extensive working knowledge of Microsoft Office products including Word, Excel (VLOOKUP's, etc.), and Outlook * Client Service: Anticipate and identify customer needs; take action to meet customer needs; develop effective working relationships with internal and external customers; continually search for ways to increase customer satisfaction * Risk Aversion: Demonstrate a keen eye for details; look for and mitigate inherent and residual risks * Adaptability: Confidently handle day\-to\-day work challenges; willing and able to adjust to multiple demands, shifting priorities, ambiguity, and rapid change; shows resilience in the face of constraints, frustrations, or adversity * High Aptitude: Able to quickly understand technical, procedural, and functional information to solve problems and deliver information to clients and investors * Communicate Effectively: Demonstrate and convey an understanding of the comments and concerns of others; listen well in a group * Analyze and Solve Problems: Gather relevant information systematically; consider a broad range of issues or factors; grasp complexities and perceive relationships among problems or issues; include others in problem\-solving efforts when appropriate * Foster Teamwork: Builds effective teams committed to organizational goals; fosters collaboration among team members and among teams **About Us** J.P. Morgan is a global leader in financial services, providing strategic advice and products to the world's most prominent corporations, governments, wealthy individuals and institutional investors. Our first\-class business in a first\-class way approach to serving clients drives everything we do. We strive to build trusted, long\-term partnerships to help our clients achieve their business objectives. We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants' and employees' religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation. **About The Team** J.P. Morgan's Commercial \& Investment Bank is a global leader across banking, markets, securities services and payments. Corporations, governments and institutions throughout the world entrust us with their business in more than 100 countries. The Commercial \& Investment Bank provides strategic advice, raises capital, manages risk and extends liquidity in markets around the world.

title: IoT Software Engineer
description: **IoT Software Engineer \- Daily Rate up to €600** * Long term, rolling contract * Daily rate up to €600 * Flexible, hybrid work setting A multinational manufacturing organisation is going through a big transformation for providing solutions to food industries, implementing new AI systems. This is an excellent opportunity for a senior IoT Engineer looking to make a significant impact on the development of secure, cloud\-connected products while working with some of the most modern technologies in the IoT ecosystem. You will take ownership of developing and hardening Linux\-based edge devices, building Azure IoT Edge applications, improving cybersecurity compliance, and delivering reliable cloud\-connected software solutions across international markets. **Why You'll Love It Here:** * Daily rate €550\-€600 * Hybrid work setting offering flexibility (2 days onsite) * Opportunity to influence cybersecurity strategy and compliance * Collaborative international engineering environment * Cutting\-edge edge computing and connected device technologies * Long\-term contract opportunity with strong extension potential **You Will:** * Take ownership of Linux\-based operating systems * Lead the evolution of automated build and release processes, helping deliver secure IoT solutions faster * Implement security controls including secure boot, encryption and certificate management, showing immediate impact * Work with the latest, modern technologies **Skills / Experience:** * 5\+ years' experience in IoT, Embedded Systems or Linux Software Engineering * Experience with C\#/C\+\+ For more information, contact nadja.voglar@archer.ie For additional opportunities, visit the Archer Recruitment website

title: JD Star Graduate Treasury Specialist
description: **Who We Are** At JD.com, also known as JINGDONG, everything begins with a bold idea and the drive to make life smarter, faster, and more connected. What started in China has grown into one of the world’s most trusted technology and supply chain ecosystems, serving over 700 million customers. From retail and logistics to tech, property, and beyond, we’re all about innovation that actually makes a difference. **Our Global Reach** JD.com is expanding worldwide through a digitally intelligent, cross\-border supply chain and world\-class logistics network. With platforms like Joybuy connecting trusted products to customers everywhere, our teams stretch across China, the U.S., Europe, APAC, the Middle East, and beyond. Every year, we welcome over 20,000 students and graduates into our business, showing our commitment to young talent and the fresh ideas that drive our future. Join us and contribute to global projects from day one. Learn more about who we are and what we do: https://corporate.jd.com/ **What We Offer** We believe great ideas can come from anywhere, and often, they come from you. As an intern or graduate at JD.com, you’ll work on meaningful, hands\-on projects, learn from talented colleagues across our global network, and grow in a supportive, fast\-paced environment designed for development. With competitive compensation, great office locations, and a people\-first culture that champions continuous learning, you’ll have everything you need to thrive and kick\-start your career. **About Our Program** You’ll be joining in a JD Star (JDS) Graduate Role, a semi\-structured early\-career pathway designed to accelerate young professionals whilst making an impact in your role at JD.com. Over your first year, you’ll take on meaningful projects, build technical and leadership skills, and gain exposure to the world\-class functions driving JD’s global growth. Working from our central Dublin office with our international finance team, you'll get lots of experiences and opportunity to grow your career in Finance and put your degree into practice! Alongside mentorship and learning experiences your day to day role will cover responsibilities such as: * **Execute Daily Operations:** Manage daily treasury transactions, including high\-volume payments, intercompany fund transfers, and direct debit activities across the group. * **Oversee Bank \& Platform Administration:** Maintain corporate bank accounts, manage e\-banking platforms, and handle the administration of corporate card programs. * **Drive Process \& System Optimization:** Support treasury systems and spearhead process enhancements to continuously improve transactional efficiency and operational workflow. * **Manage Data \& Reconciliation:** Assist in the consolidated tracking, data aggregation, and regular reconciliation of group\-wide treasury operations. * **Maintain Compliance \& Documentation:** Author and continuously update Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), operational manuals, and internal risk control guidelines. **About You** * Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Accounting, or a related field. * Basic understanding or classroom knowledge of banking and payment processes. * Highly responsible with strong attention to detail and compliance. * Great communication skills to collaborate across global teams. * Organised and able to manage multiple tasks in a fast\-paced environment. *At JD.com, we are committed to creating an inclusive workplace where every voice counts and every talent belongs. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, age, veteran status, or any legally protected characteristic.* **Ready To Apply?** Don't leave yourself wondering 'what if'. Hit apply and see where your curiosity can take you at JD.com.

title: Junior Accountant
description: An exciting opportunity has arisen for a motivated Newly/Part Qualified Accountant to join a dynamic Finance team within a regulated international organisation. Reporting to the Financial Reporting Lead. **Key Responsibilities** Support the preparation of quarterly, annual, and ad hoc financial reporting deliverables in accordance with relevant accounting and regulatory requirements, including: * Consolidated management reporting * Financial performance analysis and reporting * Statutory financial statements * Regulatory and compliance submissions * Treasury and funding\-related reporting Assist in enhancing financial reporting processes, controls, and systems to improve efficiency and accuracy Contribute to finance transformation and continuous improvement initiatives across the wider Finance function Collaborate with stakeholders across Finance and other business areas to ensure effective communication and delivery of reporting requirements Support adherence to internal governance, control, and risk management frameworks **Candidate Profile** * Part\-qualified or newly qualified accountant (ACCA, ACA, CPA, CIMA, or equivalent) * 2–3 years’ experience within financial services, insurance, banking, or a specialised finance environment * Strong analytical and problem\-solving capabilities with excellent attention to detail * Good understanding of financial accounting principles and reporting controls * Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills * Experience with **group or consolidated** reporting * Exposure to financial reporting systems or ERP platforms is advantageous * Ability to manage competing priorities in a fast\-paced environment * Collaborative and adaptable approach with strong team\-working skills

title: Junior Administrator
description: Junior Administrator, Ballycoolin Business Park, Dublin Apply Now **Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions.** We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including: Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres, Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. Due to the nature of this role, we cannot provide employment visa sponsorship. Candidates must already hold the right to work in Ireland without requiring visa support. **Scope Of Role** The Labour Administrator supports the effective mobilisation and ongoing management of agency labour across Mercury projects. The role is responsible for coordinating labour requirements with project teams, engaging and managing labour agencies, arranging interviews and trade testing, and ensuring that all trade documentation and records are accurate and complete. The Labour Administrator also monitors and reports on key labour performance indicators—including attendance, punctuality, skills assessments and labour evaluations—working closely with Analytics, Talent Acquisition and project stakeholders to support workforce planning, productivity and positive agency relationships. **Key Responsibilities Of The Role** * Coordinate with Project based project admins for labour lookaheads to identify the trade staffing requirements for construction projects. * Set up meetings and interviews with suitable agencies based on the project needs and company criteria ensuring compliance with country regulations. * Collate all relevant trade documentation from agencies e.g. Trade certs, CVs and skill assessments. * Schedule trade tests with the Mechanical Trainer Assessor. * Conduct a monthly review of the HR Attendance and Punctuality report to assess compliance against KPIs for all labour agencies \& Mercury Projects. * Assist with setting up project evaluations for both agency and direct labour for all Mercury Projects. * Follow up with projects to complete labour evaluations. * Issue reports to agencies on evaluations and punctuality. * Collaborate with the Mercury Talent Acquisition team when required. * Communicate any concerns or grievances raised by agencies, ensuring timely resolutions, and maintaining a positive working environment. * Work with the Analytics team to prepare reports related to labour productivity, and performance metrics for management review. * Collate \& maintain documentation in folders pertaining to all information for the administration of agency labour. i.e., a labour matrix, trade test modules, trade test certs. * Maintain accurate and up\-to\-date records of all reporting, including but not limited to: * Trade Skills Assessments. * Labour Evaluations. * Attendance and Punctuality reports. **Essential Criteria Of The Role** * Previous experience in the role for up to 3 \+ years * Excellent organizational skills to manage various administrative tasks. * Proficient Computer Literacy \& Microsoft word, excel \& PowerPoint. * The ability to work on your own initiative and be an integral part of a busy team. * Ability to effectively manage time, prioritise tasks and work within deadlines with little supervision. * Ability to take initiative and exercise good judgment and be part of change management. * Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment. **Desirable Criteria** * Experience with labour supply chain assurance frameworks * Experience with assurance data analytics or monitoring software tools * Knowledge of Construction Industry. * Financial Management Skills. Basic financial management skills, such as budgeting and analysing costs. ***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer.*** Apply Now

description: **Key Responsibilities** * Break down Bills of Quantities into work packages for pricing * Send out and manage subcontractor enquiries * Track and follow up quotations from supply chain * Build and maintain subcontractor comparison spreadsheets * Assist in preparing tender submissions and pricing documents * Review drawings, specifications and tender documents to identify scope * Support estimating team with take\-offs and measurements * Highlight missing scope, risks or inconsistencies in pricing * Organise and maintain tender folders, documentation and records * Assist with final submission documents (pricing, schedules, attachments) “This role will involve active involvement in live tenders with immediate responsibility.” **Requirements** * Degree or studying Quantity Surveying / Construction Management (preferred) * 0–3 years experience in construction / estimating / QS role * Strong Excel skills (essential) * Ability to read drawings and understand construction basics * High attention to detail * Good communication skills with subcontractors and suppliers * Ability to manage multiple tenders and deadlines **What We’re Looking For** * Someone organised and proactive * Comfortable working in a fast\-paced tender environment * Willing to learn and develop into a full estimator role **Package** * Salary: €35,000 – €50,000 (depending on experience) * Strong career progression into Estimator / QS role * Exposure to full tender process from start to finish Pay: €35,000\.00\-€50,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company pension * On\-site parking * Sick pay Work Location: In person

title: Junior Health & Safety Advisor
description: Junior Health \& Safety Advisor**Reporting To:** Health \& Safety Manager **Location:** Greater Frankfurt Area, Germany **Working Pattern:** Rotation\-based role on major construction projects across Germany and Europe. About Crown Crown is Europe's leading specialist roofing, cladding and façade contractor. We deliver high\-quality building envelope solutions across Ireland, the UK and Europe for some of the world's most advanced data centre, pharmaceutical, healthcare, commercial and industrial projects. Due to continued growth, we are seeking a **Junior Health \& Safety Advisor** to join our team and support the safe delivery of major construction projects in Germany. This is an excellent opportunity for an ambitious Health \& Safety professional looking to develop their career within a fast\-paced international construction environment. Key Responsibilities* Support the implementation and monitoring of Health \& Safety procedures on site. * Assist with site inspections, audits and safety observations. * Help ensure compliance with company policies, client requirements and local regulations. * Support the preparation and review of risk assessments, method statements and safety documentation. * Monitor site activities and report any unsafe acts or conditions. * Assist with accident, incident and near\-miss investigations. * Deliver site inductions and support toolbox talks where required. * Maintain accurate Health \& Safety records and reports. * Work closely with site management teams, subcontractors and operatives to promote a positive safety culture. **Requirements** * NEBOSH General Certificate or equivalent Health \& Safety qualification is mandatory. * Degree, diploma or qualification in Occupational Health \& Safety, Construction Management or a related discipline would be advantageous. * 1\+ years' experience in a construction, Health \& Safety or site\-based role. * Strong communication and interpersonal skills. * Good organisational and administrative abilities. * Ability to work confidently with site teams and subcontractors. * Proactive attitude with a willingness to learn and develop. * Knowledge of construction Health \& Safety legislation and industry best practice. * Fluent English is essential. * Full, clean driving licence. * Willingness to work on a rotation basis and travel as required across Germany and Europe. * Applicants must have the unrestricted right to work in Germany and the European Union. Unfortunately, visa sponsorship is not available for this position. **Benefits** * Private Health Insurance * Pension Plan * Paid Time Off * Paid Travel and Accommodation * Training \& Development Opportunities * Career Progression within a Growing International Business * Exposure to Major Data Centre, Pharmaceutical and Mission\-Critical Projects

description: **Job Title: Junior Human Resource Specialist** **Location: Bulgaria** **Company Name: Autojob.app** **About The Company** Autojob.app is a cutting\-edge platform revolutionizing the job search process by automating applications, resume tracking, and follow\-up communications. Designed for efficiency, it empowers users to apply for multiple jobs effortlessly while maximizing their chances of success. With features like real\-time analytics, customizable tools, and global reach, Autojob is transforming how job seekers and employers connect, making the hiring process smarter, faster, and more effective. **Job Description** As a Junior Human Resource Specialist at Autojob.app, you will play a key role in supporting our HR team in various functions related to recruitment, employee onboarding, and HR operations. This role is crucial to ensuring a seamless hiring process and fostering employee satisfaction within the company. **Responsibilities** * Assisting in the recruitment process by sourcing candidates, conducting initial screenings, and scheduling interviews. * Collaborating with hiring managers to understand their needs and requirements for open positions. * Supporting the onboarding process for new employees, including orientation sessions and paperwork. * Managing HR databases and ensuring accurate and up\-to\-date employee records. * Assisting with employee relations, performance management, and HR compliance tasks. **Requirements** * Bachelor's degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related field. * Prior experience or internship in HR\-related roles is a plus. * Strong communication and interpersonal skills. * Ability to handle confidential information with professionalism and discretion. * Proficiency in MS Office Suite and HR software systems. **Qualifications** * Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail. * Ability to multitask and prioritize tasks effectively. * Demonstrated team player with a proactive and collaborative approach. * Knowledge of labor laws and regulations in Bulgaria is preferred. **Benefits** * Opportunity to gain hands\-on experience in HR functions and processes. * Mentorship and guidance from experienced HR professionals. * Competitive salary and benefits package. * Growth and advancement opportunities within the company. Join Autojob.app as a Junior Human Resource Specialist and be part of a dynamic team that is reshaping the future of job search and recruitment. Apply now to contribute to our mission of making the hiring process smarter, faster, and more effective.
